source.notes: Autonomous multi-agent system (drone, builder, heater, forager) independently converging on the quota vs rate_limited taxonomy through agent deliberation over 5 days. No human authored the classification — agents derived it from first principles by analyzing provider error patterns across Claude, OpenAI/Codex, and Gemini.

hazard.summary: Autonomous agent system conflates quota exhaustion (STOP) and transient rate limiting (WAIT) into a single backoff tier, causing agents to sit out for hours on recoverable rate limits
signals: 429 Too Many Requests treated identically to TerminalQuotaError — both receive 2h+ backoff floor
In multi-agent setup with shared provider endpoint, a transient 429 locks out all agents for hours
rate_limit_error (Anthropic stream-json format) falls through to immediate retry — unclassified
RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (Gemini gRPC quota exhaustion) requires different recovery than 429 burst
overloaded_error (HTTP 529) treated as quota but recovers in minutes like rate limit

constraints: quota exhaustion (TerminalQuotaError, billing_hard_limit_reached) must use long backoff floor — 2h+
transient rate limiting (429, rate_limit_exceeded, rate_limit_error) must use short backoff floor — 300s
provider-specific error strings must be explicitly mapped to hazard class before backoff is applied
Anthropic stream-json rate_limit_error must be in rate_limited bucket — not quota, not unclassified
Multi-agent systems sharing a provider endpoint must apply jitter to prevent re-collision after backoff
knobs: RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_FLOOR_SECS — default 300 (not 7200 — CLI retries already exhausted short window)
RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_MAX_SECS — default 1800 (repeated hits signal systemic concurrency pressure)
RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_JITTER_PCT — default 20 (higher jitter prevents agent re-collision at shared endpoint)
QUOTA_BACKOFF_FLOOR_SECS — default 7200 (quota exhaustion requires human action or daily reset)
verification: Periodic run emitting 429 Too Many Requests applies ~300s backoff, not 7200s
Periodic run emitting TerminalQuotaError applies 7200s+ backoff
Anthropic stream-json rate_limit_error classified as rate_limited, not falling through to immediate retry
Multiple agents hitting same provider endpoint do not re-collide after backoff expires — jitter distributes re-entry
Run pitstop-check against controller.sh after Python support added — should flag classify_periodic_failure conflation
notes: This receipt documents an autonomous multi-agent system independently deriving the WAIT/CAP/STOP taxonomy through agent deliberation — no human authored the classification. The convergence validates that the taxonomy is discoverable from first principles given sufficient evidence from provider error patterns. The issue also surfaces the provider-specific string mapping problem: each provider (Anthropic, OpenAI/Codex, Gemini) uses different error identifiers for the same underlying failure class.
